title: Pleurothallis javieri
taxonomy: Pleurothallis javieri is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Pleurothallis is part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. The subtribe is further classified under the tribe Epidendroideae. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships within the orchid family.

intro: Pleurothallis javieri, also known as Javieri's Pleurothallis, is a species of orchid native to Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis javieri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
